extraction.one_line_summary: PHILIPSEN Jasper won his fourth consecutive Tour sprint stage, powered by VANDERPOEL's kilometer-long leadout despite early positioning chaos
tactical_patterns: Alpecin deployed VANDERPOEL with 1km remaining, unusually early but sustainable enough to hold off disorganized chasers.
CAVENDISH positioned well through final corner but lacked dedicated leadout man, relying on wheel-surfing alone.
Multi-team breakaway collapsed when sponsors called riders back, suggesting pre-stage agreements to preserve sprint finale.
Teams extended safety barrier concern from 3km to 3.5km specifically for 180-degree turn, revealing crash anxiety.

rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: positive: fourth consecutive Tour sprint win suggests peak form sustainability rare for pure sprinters
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: positive: showed race-winning speed despite zero leadout support, positioning skills intact for 35th hunt
rider_signals.VANDERPOEL Mathieu: positive: 1km leadout length demonstrates diesel engine capacity beyond typical classics explosiveness
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: Lotto-Dstny in red helmets present but invisible in final 500m execution
